summary: High‑energy July ’89 show with inspired playing and crisp performance; recording quality not discussed but presumed solid.
review: Both reviewers rave about the July 1989 run, calling it one of the band’s all‑time greats. The set is praised for relentless energy, standout versions of China > Rider, Built To Last, and a fiery Not Fade Away that rolls straight into a heartfelt “Bid You Goodnight” before a classic Johnny B. Goode encore. No recording‑quality details are given, but the enthusiasm suggests an enjoyable listening experience.

ai_rating.rationale: Both 5‑star reviews highlight exceptional musical performance and no noted recording flaws; strong show weight pushes rating into the high range.

recording_quality.technical_notes: No reviewers commented on sound quality, so no specific technical issues are reported.

standout_songs: China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider
show_quality.setlist_flow: The show maintains high energy from start to finish, with a seamless transition from Not Fade Away into the on‑stage “Bid You Goodnight” huddle and a rousing Johnny B. Goode encore.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Delivers an emotional outro solo and powerful vocals during the “Bid You Goodnight” moment.
band_member_comments.Keys: Brent Mydland is visibly excited, motioning during the huddle and adding his signature keyboard energy.
band_member_comments.Drums: Bill Kreutzmann reacts wildly to the crowd, driving the high‑octane feel.
